title: ugethwnd
text: ugethwnd Return the window handle of the Uniface main application window. Explain this code #include <windows.h> HWND ugethwnd (); Copy Parameters None Return Values The window handle of the calling Uniface application window. Use Microsoft Windows platforms only. In library yrtl . Description ugethwnd returns the window handle of the application window of the calling Uniface application. This handle can be used in subsequent Microsoft Windows API calls, for instance the SetWindowText function. The type HWND is defined in the include file windows.h For example, code that uses ugethwnd (see the example provided with ugetname ). Example: ugetname and ugethwnd The following example uses ugethwnd and ugetname to obtain the Uniface application window handle and title. It then changes the application title a couple of times (implementing a simple progress indicator) and finally restores it back to its original value. Explain this code #include <h3gl.h> #include <windows.h> EXPORT void PHASECOUNTER () { HWND hApp; char name[201]; char txt[50]; int i; hApp = ugethwnd(); /* Get application window handle */ ugetname(hApp, name, sizeof(name)-1); /* Get window title */ for ( i=1; i<10; i++ ) /* During 10 seconds ... */ { sprintf(txt, "Phase %d of 10", i); SetWindowText(hApp, txt); /* Change application window title */ Sleep(1000); /* Pause 1 second */ } SetWindowText(hApp, name); /* Restore application window title*/ } Copy Was this topic helpful?
